refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies teleoperation latency compatibility with safe remote operation. Refining it to a measurable latency metric (p95 ≤ 150 ms) aligns with acceptance criteria and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry data.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting for teleoperation sessions.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to reduce processing delays near vehicle and operator.
DP3: Design telemetry and tasking data pipelines with minimal buffering and efficient serialization.
DP4: Integrate latency monitoring and adaptive control mechanisms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between network protocol optimization and telemetry data handling.
Dependency on network link quality and vehicle communication hardware.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically addresses the latency requirement with minimal cross-domain coupling; other DPs can be modularized.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed baseline for latency measurement.
Risk that network QoS may be insufficient under peak load or adverse conditions.
Assumes vehicle and teleoperation modules support protocol optimizations.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 (edge computing) if network optimization alone does not meet latency targets.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation command processing, telemetry data handling, and network communication optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional telemetry data exchange between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Teleoperation receives tasking commands from NGHE Solution module.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network interface for communication with remote operator.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of latency-related functions; low diffusion as latency logic is centralized.
Vehicle handles telemetry endpoints but latency optimization logic is minimal here.
System-level coordination but no direct latency control.
Safety alerts may be triggered by latency breaches but no latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency control and optimization logic is best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and enable focused ownership.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle module telemetry interface performance.
Risk of latency logic duplication if other modules implement partial optimizations.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish clear interface contracts and monitoring to prevent diffusion of latency logic outside Teleoperation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (optimized network protocols and QoS) directly addresses the refined FR with measurable latency targets and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Potential for latency optimization logic to be duplicated in Vehicle or NGHE Solution modules if not properly controlled.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment scores and low diffusion, supporting clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry data and network monitoring within Teleoperation module boundaries.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear mapping from FR to DP to IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Define strict interface contracts to prevent latency logic diffusion.
Plan integration tests to verify end-to-end latency across modules.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a viable DP, and assigns a clear IM boundary with manageable risks and verification feasibility.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry data.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting for teleoperation sessions.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Vehicle and Teleoperation modules support protocol optimizations.
open_risks: Network QoS insufficiency under peak load.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement data under operational conditions.
Interface performance metrics between Teleoperation and Vehicle.
